cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.de.description:
schema.org Defined Term schema.

**Union-type policy:** In CDIF profile UML models an attribute typed as
schema:DefinedTerm is represented by a single attribute of that class type.
The JSON Schema implementation permits the property value to be EITHER a
plain string (interpreted as schema:name or schema:termCode in the default
vocabulary) OR a full schema:DefinedTerm object (with explicit @type, name,
identifier, termCode, inDefinedTermSet). Consumers should accept either form.

Note that this differs from the cdif Concept policy: a Concept (skos:Concept)
in CDIF profiles MUST be a controlled-vocabulary entry (object form or
@id-reference) — plain strings are not permitted for Concept-typed values
because vocabulary identity cannot be recovered from an unscoped label.

schema:name.description: text label for the term that is useful to human user

schema:inDefinedTermSet.description: Name for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.

schema:termCode.description: A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary. Analogous to skos:Notation

cdif.bbr.metadata.profiles.cdifProfile.c.title: CDIF Concept Scheme
cdif.bbr.metadata.profiles.cdifProfile.c.description: Profile module for SKOS concept schemes used as controlled vocabularies in CDIF metadata. Thin wrapper around skosConceptScheme.

@context.description: JSON-LD context declaring the skos namespace prefix and any additional prefixes used in concept URIs. May be a single object or an array of strings and objects.

@id.description: URI identifier for this concept scheme.

skos:prefLabel.description: Preferred lexical label for the concept scheme. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.

skos:altLabel.description: Alternative lexical labels (acronyms, abbreviations, spelling variants).

skos:definition.description: Formal explanation of the meaning or purpose of this concept scheme.

skos:notation.description: Classification code or notation for this concept scheme.

skos:note.description: General note about the concept scheme.

skos:hasTopConcept.description: Top-level concepts in this scheme. Each item is a skos:Concept (inline or @id reference).

@id.description: URI reference to a concept defined elsewhere

schema:version.description: Version identifier for the concept scheme.

@context.description: JSON-LD context declaring the skos namespace prefix. May be a single object or an array of strings and objects.

skos:prefLabel.description: Preferred lexical label for this concept. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.

skos:notation.description: Classification code for this concept within a scheme.

skos:definition.description: Formal explanation of the meaning of this concept.

skos:inScheme.description: Concept scheme(s) this concept belongs to.

skos:broader.description: Broader (parent) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.

skos:narrower.description: Narrower (child) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.

ConceptRef.description: Reference (by URI) to a skos:Concept defined elsewhere. Used inside skos:broader / skos:narrower as the @id-reference alternative to an inline Concept.

LanguageTaggedValue.description: An RDF literal value with a language tag, serialized as a JSON-LD value object. Inlined from skosConcept (the resolver does not preserve cross-file '#/$defs/...' fragment refs).

@language.description: BCP 47 language tag (e.g. en, fr, de).

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.va.description: Schema.org PropertyValue-based variableMeasured. Defines properties for a measured variable in a dataset, typed as schema:PropertyValue.

schema:name.description: string label associated with the variable in the dataset serialization

items.description: human intelligible name for variable that conveys semantics

schema:measurementTechnique.description: Text description or URI specifying how values for the variable were obtained.

schema:propertyID.description: identifier or name for the property concept

schema:unitCode.description: URI or code identifying the unit of measure

schema:minValue.description: minimum numeric value in the dataset

schema:maxValue.description: maximum numeric value in the dataset

anyOf.description: link to a web page useful for interpreting the variable

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.sp.description: Spatial Extent description for CDIF discovery profile.  Note required prefix declaration for geoSparql ( http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#) and OGC simple features (sf: http://www.opengis.net/ont/sf#)

schema:additionalType.description: Domain-specific type classifications for this place (e.g. facility type,laboratory classification, feature type).

schema:name.description: A place name for the location, either as a string or a DefinedTerm from a gazetteer.

schema:identifier.description: Formal identifier for this place (e.g. ROR identifier for a facility).

schema:alternateName.description: Alternate or abbreviated name(s) for this place.

schema:geo.description: Either a bounding box or a point location.

anyOf.description: A point location. Point locations are recommended for data that is associated with specific sample locations, particularly if these are widely spaced such that an enclosing bounding box would be a misleading representation of the spatial location. Be aware that some client applications might only index or display bounding box extents or a single point location.

A schema:GeoShape bounding box. The geometry is described with a set of latitude/longitude pairs (in that order).The documentation for schema:GeoShape states 'Either whitespace or commas can be used to separate latitude and longitude; whitespace should be used when writing a list of several such points.'

schema:box.description: A GeoShape box defines an area on the surface of the earth defined by point locations of the southwest corner and northeast corner of the rectangle in latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The two corner coordinate points are separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian.

anyOf.description: A schema:GeoShape curvilinear trace, for resource related to a linear trace like a ship track or airplane flight line

schema:line.description: A GeoShape box defines a curvilinear geometry as a string of latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The coordinate point pairs are also separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian. e.g. '39.33 120.77 40.44 123.96 41.00 121.34'

geosparql:hasGeometry.description: Optional geographic extent using wkt geometry, see Ocean InfoHub (https://book.oceaninfohub.org/thematics/spatial/README.html#simple-geosparql-wkt). Other geometry schemes might be specified in a specific domain profile, e.g. for atmospheric, subsurface data, or local coordinate systems. NOTE that the location specified here should be the same as the schema.org point or contained within the specified bounding box.

@type.description: MUST be sf: (<http://www.opengis.net/ont/sf#>) SimpleFeature geometry type. See https://opengeospatial.github.io/ogc-geosparql/geosparql11/sf_geometries.ttl

@value.description: a WKT geometry description string, e.g. 'POINT(-76 -18)'

@id.description: identifier string for coordinate reference system, e.g., 'CRS84'

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.te.description: The time interval during which data was collected or observations were made; or a time period that an activity or collection is linked to intellectually or thematically (for example, 1997 to 1998; the 18th century) (see https://documentation.ardc.edu.au/display/DOC/Temporal+coverage). For documentation of Earth Science, Paleobiology or Paleontology datasets, we are interested in the second case-- the time period that data are linked to thematically. NOTE-- context must include "time": "http://www.w3.org/2006/time#"}

anyOf.description: a w3c time proper interval with bounds that are named time ordinal eras with identifier, e.g. geologic age. This is a SOSO schema.org extension

schema:description.description: free text description of the temporal interval

time:intervalStartedBy.description: identifier for a named time ordinal era that is older bound of time interval, e.g. 'isc:LowerDevonian'

time:intervalFinishedBy.description: identifier for a named time ordinal era that is younger bound of time interval, e.g. 'isc:LowerDevonian'

anyOf.description: a w3c time proper interval with bounds that numeric ages.

@context.description: Must exactly match the specified @context object.

schema:description.description: free text description of the temporal interval

anyOf.description: Simple ISO8601 encoding of calendar date, dateTime, or time interval with calendar date bounds

time:hasTRS.description: identifier for a temporal reference system; default is million years before prsent as a decimal number

cdif.bbr.metadata.qualityProperties.qual.title: definitions for simple quality measure property

dqv:isMeasurementOf.description: specify the quality measure that is reported, by name, with an ID ref, or as a Defined Term

@id.description: a resolvable reference to a representation of a quality measure

dqv:value.description: the reported result of the quality measure, either as a string or a defined term from a vocabulary

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.id.description:
Properties for a schema.org identifier (schema:PropertyValue pattern).

**Union-type policy:** In CDIF profile UML models an attribute typed as
schema:Identifier / schema:PropertyValue is represented by a single attribute
of that class type. The JSON Schema implementation permits the property value
to be EITHER a plain string (interpreted as the bare identifier value) OR a
full schema:PropertyValue object (with explicit @type, propertyID, value).
Consumers should accept either form.

schema:propertyID.description: In this context for the schema:PropertyValue, this field is an identifier for the identifier schema, e.g. DOI, ARK.  Get values from https://registry.identifiers.org/registry/ for interoperability

schema:url.description: web-resolveable string for the identifier; host name part is location of a resolver that will return some representation for the given identifier value. E.g. https://doi.org/10.5066/F7VX0DMQ

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.de.description:
schema.org Defined Term schema.

**Union-type policy:** In CDIF profile UML models an attribute typed as

schema:name.description: text label for the term that is useful to human user

schema:inDefinedTermSet.description: Name for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.

schema:termCode.description: A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary. Analogous to skos:Notation

cdif.bbr.metadata.cdifDataType.cdifRefer.description: Building block to implement relations to other resources using a URL and some semantics.

@id.description: reference to a skos concept for the data type

Relation.description: DCAT Relationship surface added on top of the schema.org labeled link construct.

dcat:hadRole.description: Role of the related resource as a SKOS Concept (DCAT canonical role slot).

dcterms:relation.description: URI of the related resource (DCAT canonical pointer to what is being related to; distinct from schema:url which is the presentation URL inherited from labeledLink).

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.la.description: schema for a labeled link, a profile of schema.org/CreativeWork

title: CDIF Discovery
description: Profile module for discovery metadata. Currently a thin wrapper that composes cdifCore; reserved for discovery-specific extensions that are not part of the cdifCore foundation.

@context.description: Additional JSON-LD namespace prefixes for discovery properties.

schema:measurementTechnique.description: The technique, technology, or methodology used for measurement or determination of the dataset values.

schema:variableMeasured.description: What does the dataset measure? (e.g., temperature, pressure)

dqv:hasQualityMeasurement.description: Quality measurements reported to assess the resource.

@id.description: reference to a skos concept for the data type
